title: Quay.io container images are outdated
body:
**Description**

On the buildah.io [release notes to the latest release](https://buildah.io/releases/2022/05/09/Buildah-version-v1.26.1-mini.html) it is noted that container images should be available at Quay
> In addition, container images will be available at
> https://quay.io/repository/buildah/stable and
> https://quay.io/repository/containers/buildah

However both repos are severely outdated with the latest release available being v1.23.3. Even if you argue that it says `*will be* available`, it's been three weeks since the post was created. Is there no automated release pipeline for this?

**Describe the results you received:**

Outdated buildah images

**Describe the results you expected:**

Images of current buildah versions

comments.body: Hi @der-eismann , Thanks for creating the issue and pointing this out. Could you use `quay.io/buildah/upstream:latest` till this is fixed in upstream. @cevich Could you please take a look ? I am not sure why `quay.io/containers/buildah` and `buildah/stable` is not updated.

@der-eismann thanks for the report.  FWIW, there's generally a couple of weeks delay from the time a release gets posted on our release page and it finally lands in quay.io.  That's because the process that builds that image first checks to find which image is considered "stable" on the [Fedora Updates System](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/) and then some adjustments for the version(s) of Fedora.

I see that v1.23.3 is stable on Fedora 35, whereas the versions after that are stable on Fedora 36 and 37.  So I'm guessing the logic that takes into consideration which version of Fedora to use might not be using past Fedora 35 at the moment and that likely needs adjusting.  However, I'm not seeing that code quickly, but I bet @cevich will find it to at least confirm the theory or give us the real dirt.
The image should be built off of F36 at this point.

>The image should be built off of F36 at this point.

Agree, something must be broken with the build process...yep, it's [been broken for the last 6 days.](https://github.com/containers/buildah/actions/workflows/multi-arch-build.yaml) for the same reason the podman images are broken and [likely needs a similar fix.](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14437).

I've also got a PR open to [move the build process into Cirrus-CI](https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4019) where we'll notice it's broken a lot sooner.  In any case, I'll work on a fix for this as well.

Actually, @TomSweeneyRedHat while I work on #4019 (it won't fix the `sed` problem) do you think you could help apply the `sed`, fix to the Dockerfile's here?

Clarification: A `Containerfile` rename and/or mass-cleanup would be nice too, but aren't strictly necessary to get builds rolling again.
@cevich, tried to get to it today but lost the battle.  Will try to do it tomorrow.

Thanks for investigating and working on it everyone! 
On another note, the [1.26 blog post](https://buildah.io/releases/2022/05/09/Buildah-version-v1.26.1-mini.html) says:
> We are shipping this release on Fedora 35, Fedora 36, and RHEL 8.6.0.2.

This is wrong for Fedora 35 as [buildah is still on 1.23.3/4 there](https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/buildah) and I can't find anything on Google about a RHEL version 8.6.0.2. Is this some kind of internal versioning or is there a public roadmap to anticipate when the release is happening?

The only part of that I can respond do is Fedora, the RHEL images are build (and delivered?) via a totally separate system.  What you say about F35 is likely true (I haven't checked).  The build system is running based off of `fedora:latest`.  The stable image tag is set based on querying the version of buildah actually installed inside the container.  So the tags are continuously moving forward, with past image tags (based on Fedora N-1) left as-is.  Hopefully that makes sense.

> tried to get to it today but lost the battle. Will try to do it tomorrow.

No worries, you should see my browser tab collection.  It would excite even the prehistoric archaeologists :rofl:
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
